speaker: Narrator
text:
Blanche was in a panic. She had spent the whole day scrounging the area for everything she needed for a Flying Jacob casserole with rice. 
	
	If things turned out well, there'd be a dessert, cream made with all the leftover dairy her mother had left in the refrigerator.

speaker: Narrator
text:
With a fateful knock at the door, Blanche looks at Christina. She remembers the feeling of hands on her body and how deep that tongue seemed to go. 
	
	There was a certain danger in just being around her.

speaker: Blanche
text: There's some food at the table.

speaker: Blanche
text: I want to talk over dinner first.

speaker: Narrator
text:
Blanche serves a helping of Flying Jacob alongside steaming hot rice from her imported Thai rice cooker. That and a cup of the finest Thai coffee. 
	
	The two girls sit down across from each other, afraid of making eye contact. Christina picks at her food as if she's being forced to eat a fried boot.

speaker: Christina
text: I'll chew it and swallow it just like I always did!

speaker: Pasaya
text: You'll choke to death without me.

speaker: Pasaya
text: I am your body, you will listen to me.

speaker: Pasaya
text: I can't trust you to take care of yourself.

speaker: Blanche
text: I know you've had this before.

speaker: Blanche
text: I mean, I'm not the best cook in the world, but is it so unappealing that you won't take a bite?

speaker: Narrator
text: Christina puts down the fork. She lifts up the plate and tosses it aside, shattering it on the ground. Pasaya took full control of Christina's body.

speaker: Narrator
text:
Blanche gets up and storms over to Christina, grabs her by the arm in a attempt to drag her out of the house. But she gets caught off guard that Christina doesn't even budge. 
	
	Christina stands up seemingly unaware of the effort Blanche is putting on her body. Christina pushes her down quite easily.
